capabilities: Establish a scalable design system by systematically defining UI components, standardizing design tokens, and maintaining comprehensive documentation.

procedure.action: Specify UI components by creating a component library using Storybook; ensure each component is reusable and adheres to accessibility standards WCAG 2.1 AA.
Standardize design tokens by implementing a centralized token management system using Style Dictionary; ensure tokens are platform-agnostic and version-controlled.

Document guidelines by generating a living style guide using tools like Zeroheight; include usage examples, code snippets, and accessibility considerations for each component using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Compose the design system with development workflows by using CI/CD pipelines to automatically update and deploy changes to the design system repository.

Conduct regular audits of the design system by running automated tests with Jest and Axe to ensure compliance with accessibility and design standards using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

summary: Procedure to create scalable design systems by defining components, standardizing tokens, and documenting guidelines.
title: Scalable Design System Creation

failureConditions: Inconsistent UI when components are not reusable across platforms, causing increased maintenance overhead.
Design token conflicts when tokens are not standardized, leading to visual discrepancies across applications.
Outdated documentation when guidelines are not regularly updated, causing confusion among developers and designers.
